title: Match the Opposite Properties — Companion Pair Game
description: A two-column tap-to-match activity where students find OPPOSITE property pairs. Students tap a left property chip to select it, then tap its opposite on the right. Correct opposite pairs lock green with a reason and a coloured SVG Bézier connector line. Wrong taps shake red and clear. An instructions banner, animated 'opposites' badge, score pill, progress bar, confetti, and summary card complete the experience.

additionalProps.title: Match the Opposite Properties
additionalProps.subtitle: Each property on the left has an OPPOSITE on the right — tap a word to select it, then tap its opposite

pairs.reason: Hard & Soft are OPPOSITE texture properties — one resists compression, the other yields easily.
Lustrous & Dull are OPPOSITES describing surface shine — metals are lustrous; wood and paper are non-lustrous.
Transparent & Opaque are OPPOSITES — transparent lets you see through it; opaque blocks all light completely.
Soluble & Insoluble are OPPOSITES — soluble materials dissolve in water; insoluble ones remain separate.
Dense & Fluid are OPPOSITES — dense describes tightly packed matter; fluid flows freely with no fixed shape.
Heavy & Light are OPPOSITE properties of mass — objects with more mass feel heavier.

schema.description: All agent-configurable content lives inside additionalProps. Every pair must represent true OPPOSITE properties so the instructions banner and 'opposites' badge remain accurate.

title.description: Heading shown in the purple header bar. Should communicate that the task is about finding opposites.

subtitle.description: Instruction line shown beneath the heading. Should reinforce the 'opposite' concept.
subtitle.default: Each property on the left has an OPPOSITE on the right — tap a word to select it, then tap its opposite

rightColumnOrder.description: Ordered list of right-column words. Must match the 'right' field of every pair. Controls the shuffled display order in Column II — set this to a different order than the left column so students must actively think.

pairs.description: Array of left–right OPPOSITE word pairs. Every pair must be a genuine conceptual opposite so the instructions banner ('Hard is the opposite of Soft') remains accurate for the subject.

left.description: Word shown in Column I — Properties.

right.description: OPPOSITE word shown in Column II — Opposite Properties.

reason.description: One-line explanation revealed on correct match. Recommended format: 'Left & Right are OPPOSITES — explanation sentence.' The word OPPOSITES should appear so the feedback reinforces the concept.

ui_behaviour.interaction_model: tap-to-select then tap-to-match (no drag required — works on touch devices)
new_in_this_version: Animated 'opposites' badge with two-way arrows icon sits next to the header title — pulses gently to draw attention.
Instructions banner between header and game area shows a concrete example: Hard ↔ is the opposite of ↔ Soft so students know the task before touching anything.
Column II header now reads 'Column II — Opposite Properties' instead of 'Companions'.
Center gap between columns shows a small 'opposites' label above the SVG lines.
Hint bar uses opposite-specific language: 'Now tap its OPPOSITE property on the right →'.
Matched tag on left chip reads 'opposite: Soft' instead of just 'Soft'.
Cross-reference tag on right chips reads '↔ opposite of (i)' instead of '↔ (i)'.
Summary card title reads 'All Opposites Matched!' and shows an 'opposite' badge between each pair in the grid.
All default pair reasons now start with 'X & Y are OPPOSITE…' to reinforce the concept on every reveal.
correct_feedback: Companion tag pops in below reading 'opposite: [word]'
Reason slides in italicised — starts with 'X & Y are OPPOSITES…'
SVG Bézier line animates from left endpoint to right endpoint with colour-coded dots
wrong_feedback: Right chip shakes and flashes red (#fef2f2 background)
ui_behaviour.right_column_opacity: 0.55 when no left chip is selected, 1.0 when a selection is active — focuses the student on available targets
ui_behaviour.summary_card: Full-screen gradient overlay with confetti, trophy, 'All Opposites Matched!' title, score/attempt count, 2-col pair grid (left badge ↔ 'opposite' badge ↔ right badge + reason), Play Again button
ui_behaviour.hint_bar: Context-sensitive — 'Tap a property on the left, then find its opposite on the right!', 'Now tap its OPPOSITE property on the right →', or '{n} of {total} opposites matched'

agent_readme.overview: CompanionPairGame is a two-column OPPOSITE-matching widget. Students click a left chip (property word) to select it (glows, scales up), then click a right chip (its opposite). The UI consistently uses the word 'opposite' across the badge, instructions banner, column header, hint bar, matched tags, and summary — so every interaction reinforces the concept. Correct pairs lock with an animated SVG connector line. Wrong taps shake and clear.
architecture_notes: Single .tsx file, all inline styles, no Tailwind.
Keyframes injected via useEffect into document.head with unique id 'cpg-kf-v2' — cleaned up on unmount.
Two new keyframes added: cpgBadgePop (used for the 'opposite' tag in summary) and cpgOppositeGlow (used for the header badge pulse).
Custom OppositeIcon SVG component (two curved opposite arrows) used in header badge, instructions banner, hint bar, and matched tags — no external icon dependency.

Instructions banner is always visible above the matching area and is hard-coded to show the first pair (pairs[0].left ↔ pairs[0].right) as the example — ensure pairs[0] is a clear, student-friendly opposite.
SVG lines recalculated via useRef DOM measurements in recalcLines(), called after each match with two setTimeout passes (120 ms and 420 ms) to handle hint-block expand/collapse layout shifts.
rightColumnOrder controls Column II order independently of Column I.
setStepDetails() called once on mount: currentMode: 'practice', isPaused: true.
stopAutoNext / setStopAutoNext props accepted but not actively used.
content_guidelines: Every pair MUST be a genuine conceptual opposite — the instructions banner will display pairs[0] as the example 'X is the opposite of Y'.
Reason strings should begin with 'X & Y are OPPOSITES — …' to match the reinforcement pattern used throughout the UI.
rightColumnOrder should differ from the left column order to prevent positional guessing.
Keep pair count between 2 and 10; the layout scrolls vertically for larger sets.

additionalProps.title: Electricity — Match the Opposites
additionalProps.subtitle: Each electrical property on the left has an OPPOSITE on the right — find all 4 pairs!

pairs.reason: Conductor & Insulator are OPPOSITES — one allows current flow, the other blocks it.
Positive & Negative are OPPOSITE electric charge types.
Series & Parallel are OPPOSITE circuit configurations.
Voltage & Resistance are OPPOSITES — voltage drives current; resistance opposes it.

additionalProps.title: States of Matter — Match the Opposites
additionalProps.subtitle: Each state or change on the left has an OPPOSITE on the right — find all 3 pairs!

pairs.reason: Solid & Gas are OPPOSITES — solid has fixed shape and volume; gas has neither.
Melting & Freezing are OPPOSITE changes of state — solid→liquid vs liquid→solid.
Evaporation & Condensation are OPPOSITES — liquid→gas vs gas→liquid.

additionalProps.title: Cell Organelles — Match the Function
additionalProps.subtitle: Each organelle on the left has a matching function on the right — find all 4 pairs!

pairs.reason: Mitochondria & Energy production are LINKED — mitochondria produce ATP, the cell's fuel.
Ribosome & Protein synthesis are LINKED — ribosomes translate mRNA into proteins.
Lysosome & Digestion are LINKED — lysosomes break down waste using enzymes.
Vacuole & Storage are LINKED — vacuoles store water, nutrients, and waste.

decision_tree.default_chapter6: Pass no additionalProps — renders the 6 Chapter-6 science opposite property pairs.
decision_tree.custom_opposites: Provide pairs[] where every left–right pairing is a genuine opposite. Set reason strings to start with 'X & Y are OPPOSITES — …'.
decision_tree.function_matching: If pairs are not true opposites (e.g. organelle→function), adjust the title/subtitle/reason wording accordingly and note that the header 'opposites' badge and banner will still show — communicate that to students.
decision_tree.fewer_pairs: 2–4 pairs for a short warm-up activity.
decision_tree.more_pairs: Up to 10 pairs; the layout scrolls vertically inside the card.

error_handling.missing_pairs: Falls back to DEFAULT_PAIRS (6 Chapter-6 science opposite pairs).
error_handling.rightColumnOrder_mismatch: If a word in rightColumnOrder has no matching pair.right, it is silently filtered out.
error_handling.duplicate_right_words: Each 'right' value must be unique; duplicates will cause SVG lines to share an endpoint.
error_handling.empty_reason: If reason is an empty string the reason line renders blank — always supply a reason starting with 'X & Y are OPPOSITES — …'.
error_handling.non_opposite_pairs: If pairs are not genuine opposites, the instructions banner ('X is the opposite of Y') will be misleading — ensure content accuracy.

instructions_for_student: Tap a property word on the left to select it, then find its OPPOSITE on the right and tap it. A green connector line and a fun fact appear when you get it right — a red shake tells you to try again. Match all opposites to win!
